text: Standing just outside a glossy glass enclosure, my reflection half-forms in the pane as I peer in. The world feels clinical, deliberate, with every surface engineered for function. The promise of ideas being born in 'R+D' tugs at my curiosity. Entering, words upon the wall lure me deeper: here, thought is made manifest, innovation tangible. The text's introspection mirrors my own--the presence of surveillance, the hum of activity, the latent anxiety of being observed. I wonder, in these spaces dedicated to progress, what is lost in the act of constant watching? I feel both inspired and unsettled, welcomed and scrutinized all at once.
